Kids formulate what they will do in their teens around 14 or so years old so jobs that come up when they are 25 aren't something they are interested in or even qualified for because those jobs didn't exist when they were kids and could build a passion and qualification for. the reality is when i was going through school, if i had followed what my dad did, i'd be fucking broke because the reality is they aren't hiring for anyone like that until trump so i would have been out of a job for nearly a decade or been laid off. The reality is though you need to develop and economy that exist for teens as they move into being adults to fill them.

Kenya's stock market has reached a record Sh3.8 trillion in market value, up 28% since January. Foreign investors earned 19% dollar returns in the first six months of the year.

If the reports are true, this is a heartbreaking tragedy. A young man allegedly mobilizes fellow youths for a political event, they are reportedly offered just KSh 10,000 to share among about 60 people after spending the day singing praises, a dispute follows, and he allegedly never returns alive. No Kenyan's life should end over politics, patronage or KSh 167. The circumstances surrounding this death must be investigated independently, transparently and without fear or favour. Those responsible, whoever they are, must be held accountable. May Cecil rest in peace.
